blog_title: Shop These 5 Popular Things For Sure When You Are In Goa
seo_title: Shop These 5 Popular Things For Sure When You Are In Goa
description: Discover the ultimate travel packing list for every adventure. Pack smart, light, and stress-free with essential tips .
body_content:
Published by travelxploria on Tue Jun 17 2025

With its breathtaking beaches, waterfalls, and countless stories, Goa is such a place that will make you fall in love with it again and again. Visiting there, you will turn into an absolute shopaholic (even if you aren’t). The chic boutiques, local bazaars, and hippie flea markets will make you go crazy with wonder and temptation. The traditional craftsmen working there create magic, and the art sold speaks volumes. When in Goa, you must invest in its local art and crafts.

A renowned travel and tour agency offers the cheapest tour packages for Goa! Do your homework to stop by the best one.

Given below is a list of some of the most popular things that you can’t help but buy:

The Kunbi Saree:

When you close your eyes and imagine Goa, a saree isn’t something that you would see. However, did you know that the Kunbi saree is an essential part of the Goan folk culture? Coming in checks of white and red, they are worn by the Kunbi tribal women of Goa. These people live in the hinterlands of the state and their culture, especially this amazing fabric, is almost on the verge of vanishing. Once you buy it, you need to wear it by tying a dentil knot over the shoulder.

The Artwork of Mario Miranda:

Any art lover would admit that with his elegant art, Mario Miranda has made Goa more famous. The seamless stories, lively characters, and bold black lines of his paintings will cast a spell on you. His paintings mostly symbolize the Goan laid-back life with the Goan houses, fishermen, and so on. You can’t say adieu to the place without buying a Mario Miranda art piece.


Bamboo Craftwork:


Visiting Goa, you will find different kinds of bamboo craftwork. To enhance your home’s beauty, you can find intricately woven lamps, bags, baskets, and storage items. You can visit stores like Cane Craft in Panjim or you can go to the Mapusa Market as the craftsmen set up shops there as well. If you are someone who would like to go for something more extraordinary, then in smaller villages, there are also several amazing bamboo crafts available, but it might be hard to find these without the help of the locals.

Local Spices:

Are you a foodie? Do you like to eat as well as cook? If yes, then you must get your hands on the Goan spices. They are the best in India. Nutmegs, dried mangoes, and Triphala add to local dishes a lip-smacking taste. Other than these, do consider purchasing cardamom, cinnamon, cloves, and peppers from this spice haven. The popular Mapusa Market provides spice powders, dry spices, and other fascinating flavors. Take a look around and get what catches your fancy or what you will need the most to make some awesome dishes back home.

Goan Cashew Feni:

One of Goa’s most famous things to buy is the GoanCashew Feni. It’s the local alcohol that is made from the cashew apple, something that is harvested only during the summer. The delicious fruity liquor contains about 40 – 42% alcohol content and tastes even better when combined with Limca.
